title: Model-invoke type
description: Configure type = MODEL_INVOKE for provider-neutral operation bindings and model operations through a MODEL_WORKER backend.
openingSummary: MODEL_INVOKE is the WorkstationType for provider-neutral operation and operationBindings. Set type to MODEL_INVOKE when a step must run through a MODEL_WORKER with those operation surfaces, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords on MODEL_WORKSTATION instead.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type MODEL_INVOKE and bind a MODEL_WORKER. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so model outcomes route correctly. Set operation for the provider-neutral operation name, and use operationBindings when this step needs named binding maps. Do not set promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, stopWords, openCodeAgent, classificationRoutes, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose MODEL_WORKSTATION for prompt-file and outcome-parse surfaces, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference harness dispatch. MODEL_INVOKE is not a scheduling behavior and is not MODEL_WORKSTATION — keep promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords on the model-workstation type.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with MODEL_INVOKE overlay applicability. MODEL_INVOKE selects the exclusive operation and operationBindings fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a MODEL_INVOKE with behavior STANDARD, an operation, and optional operationBindings. The misuse example borrows outcomeFormat from MODEL_WORKSTATION — that field is excluded from MODEL_INVOKE.

links.companionIntro: MODEL_INVOKE requires a MODEL_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the model worker, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. MODEL_WORKSTATION also uses MODEL_WORKER but selects prompt/parse surfaces instead of operation fields.
links.misuseNote: outcomeFormat belongs on MODEL_WORKSTATION. Keep MODEL_INVOKE on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, routing fields, and operation / operationBindings only.
